title: Victim identified in fatal Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: The victim of the fatal Roxbury hit-and-run has been identified as 47-year-old Steven Stewart from Worcester.
Stewart was killed on March 20 after being hit near Southampton and Bradston Streets in Roxbury.
Boston police stated no arrests have been made, and the investigation into the incident continues.

title: Mike Vrabel reacts after TreVeyon Henderson lends support to Bulls guard cut after anti-LGBTQ+ statements
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel addressed TreVeyon Henderson's social media posts supporting Jaden Ivey, emphasizing player education and inclusivity.
TreVeyon Henderson posted Bible verses in support of Jaden Ivey after Ivey's anti-LGBTQ+ comments led to his release by the Bulls.
Vrabel stressed players must balance personal beliefs with their role in representing the Patriots organization as a whole.

title: This Concord mom is making her dream come true by running Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kelly Turner from Concord, 43, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Turner's marathon dream was delayed due to parenting responsibilities and battling Lyme disease.
The New England Patriots Foundation supports nonprofits focused on education, youth development, and health in underserved communities.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her best friend
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Brette Miller is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her best friend Angelle Lozano.
Lozano died at age 29 from a ruptured brain aneurysm; Miller supports The Brain Aneurysm Foundation.
One in 50 people in the U.S. has an unruptured aneurysm; 30,000 U.S. rupture cases occur annually.

title: Clover Food Lab puts itself on the market
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Clover Food Lab is looking for a buyer after stabilizing its business post-bankruptcy.
The company announced plans to lay off 182 workers, complying with state law requirements.
Clover operates 11 restaurants, a meal box delivery service, and a catering business.

title: ‘BPS transportation is failing us’: School bus delays have Boston parents calling for change
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston Public Schools buses average 93% on-time in the morning and 88% in the afternoon, below the 95% target.
Approximately 1,330 students arrive late in the morning and 2,280 are delayed in the afternoon daily due to bus issues.
BPS faces challenges like driver shortages and mechanical issues, with parents experiencing frequent delays and cancellations.

title: How did the ‘Seger bar’ guy get Gov. Healey for his latest video?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey appeared in Brian Daly's viral video series evaluating bars on playing Bob Seger music.
Healey joined Daly at Beacon Hill's 21st Amendment bar, enjoyed a Guinness, and shared a Seger concert anecdote.
Daly met Healey at a union event last year and coincidentally encountered her again while filming in 2026.

title: Michelin-recommended dumpling chain Nan Xiang to open in Framingham
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Nan Xiang Soup Dumplings plans to open its first Massachusetts location in Framingham later this year.
The new restaurant will occupy the former Fresh Market space at 84 Worcester Rd., focusing on handmade soup dumplings.
Nan Xiang Express already operates locations in Boston's Chinatown and Shrewsbury, offering fast-casual dining.

title: ‘The Drama’ review: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson’s darkly funny Boston movie is designed to discomfort
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Drama', filmed in Boston, features Zendaya and Robert Pattinson exploring themes of societal norms and red flags.
Director Kristoffer Borgli uses humor and discomfort to highlight issues of cancel culture and societal transgressions.
The film's exploration of relationship dynamics is set against Boston's backdrop, with scenes shot at local landmarks.

title: National UMass survey has bad news for Trump
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Trump's approval rating drops to 33% in a UMass Amherst poll, the lowest of his second term.
The poll highlights economic concerns as a major driver, with 71% disapproving of Trump's handling of inflation.
Support wanes among key voter groups, with a decline in approval from men, Black voters, and independents.
